departments.concept: Organizational units of the clinic. Use this for macro-level analysis of hospital operations, patient flow by wing, and departmental workload distribution.

columns_with_descriptions.name: Clinical division name (e.g., 'Emergency', 'Radiology', 'Pediatrics'). USE THIS for categorical business grouping.
columns_with_descriptions.location: Geographic location of the department within the hospital facility.

staff.concept: Hospital human resources. Use this to analyze practitioner performance, track doctor-patient ratios, and resource allocation across departments.

columns_with_descriptions.first_name: Given name of the clinician or administrator.
columns_with_descriptions.role: Professional designation (e.g., 'Doctor', 'Specialist', 'Registered Nurse'). Use this to filter clinical vs. administrative queries.
columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Establishes the primary work-unit of the staff member.

patients.concept: Central clinical registry. This is the root table for all patient-centric metrics, demographics, and longitudinal health tracking.

columns_with_descriptions.id: [PK] Unique clinical identifier for the patient.
columns_with_descriptions.date_of_birth: Legal birth date [DATE]. Crucial for age-based clinical bucketing and pediatrics vs. geriatrics analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.nhi_number: National Health Index value; the unique medical record key for this individual.
columns_with_descriptions.gender: Biological demographic (e.g., 'Male', 'Female'). Used for gender-specific health screening analysis.

appointments.concept: Clinical encounters and utilization events. This table links patients to doctors and departments. Use this to determine hospital visit frequency and service use.

columns_with_descriptions.id: [PK] Unique record of the clinical encounter.
columns_with_descriptions.patient_id: [FK -> patients.id] Links the visit to a specific individual.
columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Tracks which clinical unit hosted the visit.
columns_with_descriptions.appointment_date: Timestamp of the clinical interaction. Used for longitudinal trend analysis (Monthly/Quarterly growth).
columns_with_descriptions.status: Outcome of the scheduled visit ('Scheduled', 'Completed', 'Cancelled'). Use 'Completed' for reality-based revenue and clinical metrics.

billing.concept: Financial health and revenue cycle management. Use this to calculate hospital income, outstanding liabilities (Pending), and departmental profitability.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Links financial charges to a clinical visit.
columns_with_descriptions.amount: The monetary value of the service [NUMERIC]. Sum this for revenue; compare with status for debt analysis.
columns_with_descriptions.status: Payment state ('Paid', 'Pending'). 'Pending' items represent accounts receivable (unpaid medical debt).
columns_with_descriptions.payment_method: Financial instrument / mode of payment used
columns_with_descriptions.invoice_date: Tax/Accounting date for the billing event.

medical_supplies.concept: Inventory and logistical management. Use this for supply-chain analysis, consumable stock monitoring, and departmental overhead tracking.

columns_with_descriptions.department_id: [FK -> departments.id] Assigns ownership and responsibility for supply stock.

lab_results.concept: Diagnostic output and pathology. Use this to identify clinical risks, track biometric trends, and screen for 'Abnormal' patient populations.

columns_with_descriptions.appointment_id: [FK -> appointments.id] Contextualizes the test within a specific hospital visit.
columns_with_descriptions.result_value: The objective numerical biological metric recorded.
columns_with_descriptions.is_abnormal: Categorical flag ('Yes', 'No'). Queries for 'patients at risk' should filter for 'Yes' in this column.
